reports.notes:
Game runs but with audio hiccups. 
Visual glitches especially with the lower body of the player character

Game loads up, and runs decently with moderate slowdown and audio hiccups.

On initial run, the main menu may load weirdly, but is usually fixed in subsequent runs.

Videos work fine, but lack any form of audio, and some in-engine cutscenes may also have issues with audio. 

Visual glitches occur, especially with the lower body of the player character when standing still.

Game crashes occasionally, usually when entering new sections of the story. Intro runs decently, leading up to the first memory.

Game also crashes at a specific point in the tutorial,(screenshot attached where Ezio is looking at Altair’s ghost on the wooden brackets) 

So far, using a save from later on doesn’t seem to work well as later areas induce massive slowdowns with the increasing number of buildings and NPCS.

title: Alan Wake
notes: This is a challenging 3D game that pushes the boundaries. Game Hangs or freezes a few times after starting and selecting Dark/Brightness setting, before you can get in game. Menu text is blurred and not readable making navigation challenging. The trick that seemed to allow it to load in game is navigating to Settings and manually changing the Brightness settings before trying to start. Then back out and start the game. When you first get in game it takes a few seconds for the world to load in, causing colorful or blank areas. In game the FPS is low average around 10-15 FPS. However despite these issues the game does run and is playable.

title: DBZ Budokai HD
notes: As of the new update, Budokai 3’s Dragon Universe mode no longer shows a black screen. Enemy characters are now visible. Budokai 3 runs great. In Budokai 1, the character select screen still has texture glitches, but the game is no longer a black screen during battles.

reports.notes: As of the new update, Budokai 3’s Dragon Universe mode no longer shows a black screen. Enemy characters are now visible. Budokai 3 runs great. In Budokai 1, the character select screen still has texture glitches, but the game is no longer a black screen during battles.
Loads into game selection and crashes.

In my case I had to convert the dumped game’s ISO file using extract-iso. The game itself is a launcher for each .xex file for the individual games. Each game must be manually booted by pressing the + button in XeniOS and selecting the respective folder for each title (DBZ1 for Budokai 1 and DBZ3 for Budokai 3). Both games have good performance. Budokai 1 has more graphical issues than Budokai 3. In the character select screen for Budokai 1, there are graphical glitches with the characters’ artwork. When entering a battle, a black screen appears.  For Budokai 3, a black screen appears when entering Dragon Universe mode. During battles, the enemy character is invisible and only appears when using a blast attack (ex. Kamehameha), teleporting, activating Hyper Mode, or triggering Dragon Rush.  Overall, both games run very well, with Budokai 3 having fewer issues.

all.notes: As of the new update, Budokai 3’s Dragon Universe mode no longer shows a black screen. Enemy characters are now visible. Budokai 3 runs great. In Budokai 1, the character select screen still has texture glitches, but the game is no longer a black screen during battles.

Game technically functions and can start, move and fight, but there are no visuals aside from text and the pause menu.

Loading screen seems to be fine.
